title: Caramelized Greek Pumpkin Stir-fry with Tiger Prawns
description: A nutritious and adventurous low-carb-friendly greek dish featuring tiger prawns, pumpkin, and other aromatic vegetables for a delightful nutty and velvety taste.

ingredients.name: Pumpkin
ingredients.notes: Use any kind of pumpkin, but a smaller variety works best.

ingredients.name: Tiger Prawns
ingredients.notes: Frozen tiger prawns work well and can be defrosted before cooking.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Use white or yellow onions for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Freshly minced garlic adds a nice punch of flavor.

ingredients.name: Red Bell Pepper
ingredients.notes: Use mild varieties for the best texture and taste.

ingredients.name: Pomegranate Molasses
ingredients.notes: For added depth of flavor and caramelization.

ingredients.name: Low-Sodium Chicken Broth
ingredients.notes: Use for a more savory base, or water if vegan option is preferred.

ingredients.name: Kalamata Olives
ingredients.notes: For a touch of Mediterranean flair.

ingredients.name: Pomegranate Seeds
ingredients.notes: To garnish and enhance the festive look.

ingredients.name: Fresh Basil Leaves
ingredients.notes: For both garnishing and adding extra freshness to the dish.

instructions: Slice pumpkin into thin rounds and lightly spray with olive oil, then place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast for about 15 minutes or until tender.
Wash, peel, and dice onion; mince garlic finely and slice zucchini.
In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic, diced onions, and sliced zucchini. Cook for about 5 minutes until vegetables soften slightly.
Add tiger prawns to the pan with enough space between them so they can cook evenly. Season lightly with salt and cook until opaque and pink, around 2-3 minutes per side.
Fold in roasted pumpkin slices, drizzle pomegranate molasses over everything, stir well, and let it simmer for about 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld together.
In a separate pan, fry sliced red bell peppers (if not already cooked), add low-sodium chicken broth or water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until liquid is reduced by half.
Pour the strained broth mixture over the pumpkin-prawn mix in the main pan. Stir well to combine all ingredients evenly.
Add pomegranate seeds and fresh basil leaves on top of the dish for garnish before serving if desired.
Serve hot with additional low-carb crackers or bread, if you prefer.
